methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $600M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Isomorphic Labs, a Series A biotech and health company founded by Demis Hassabis, has raised $600M to date. Valuation analysis triangulates against a peer universe of 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage peers in the same sector, including Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M).

Two methods are applicable to establish value. Stage-progression anchors to the $600M raised proxy and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement to next stage, implying a $1.2B valuation. Peer-median analysis across 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $805M, with the peer range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied; the sector median P/S multiple for biotech and health is 7.4x across three tickers, but the target's revenue is not on file and private companies rarely disclose this metric.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $684M to $1.4B, with a median of $1.0B. This range reflects the material dispersion in peer valuations at adjacent stages. Key uncertainties include the strength of the target's proprietary technology relative to funded peers and the timing and terms of the next financing, both of which could shift implied value materially within or beyond this band.
